completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `tests/test_tools_types.py` added with at least 12 tests covering Pydantic validation for CapabilityDescriptor (valid + invalid agent_id pattern + unknown risk_level), CapabilityToolSummary, WebResult (score bounds), CalendarEvent (end>=start validator), DispatchError (category literal).
completion_promises.evidence: tests/test_tools_types.py created with 35 collected tests (>= 12 minimum). TestCapabilityDescriptorValidation parametrises 6 invalid agent_id values (leading digit, leading hyphen, uppercase, underscore, dot, whitespace) plus a valid construction case and an invalid trust_tier case. TestCapabilityToolSummaryValidation includes valid construction, unknown risk_level rejection ('catastrophic'), and empty tool_name rejection. TestWebResultBounds parametrises score bounds (out-of-range -0.0001/-1.0/1.0001/5.0 rejected; 0.0/0.5/1.0 accepted) and rejects empty url. TestCalendarEventValidator covers end<start rejection, end==start acceptance, and end>start acceptance. TestDispatchErrorCategoryLiteral parametrises all six canonical category Literals plus rejects an unknown 'not_a_category' value. All 35 tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `as_prompt_block` byte-equal assertion against DM-tool-types.md §"Prompt-block shape" example.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAsPromptBlockByteEqual.test_as_prompt_block_byte_equal_to_dm_tool_types_example constructs the architect-agent CapabilityDescriptor with the exact role, description (with embedded newline), cost_signal='moderate', latency_signal='5-30s', trust_tier='specialist', and the two CapabilityToolSummary instances (run_architecture_session/read_only and draft_adr/mutating) from DM-tool-types.md §'Prompt-block shape'. Asserts as_prompt_block() == expected where expected is the verbatim block from the design doc (header line, blank line, multi-line description, blank line, 'Tools:' header, two indented tool lines with 4-space continuation indent for the embedded newline). A companion test_as_prompt_block_invocation_is_deterministic verifies repeat calls return identical strings.

Test file imports pytest and pydantic.ValidationError plus unittest.mock.patch and unittest.mock.MagicMock. TestPromptBlockMockInteractions exercises the pairing: test_as_prompt_block_called_once_per_descriptor uses patch.object(..., autospec=True) to mock the bound method and assert call shape, and test_double_newline_join_pattern_with_mocked_blocks uses MagicMock(spec=CapabilityDescriptor) to verify the join pattern that the supervisor prompt uses to fill {available_capabilities}. Tests are collected and run with pytest; all 35 pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] No tests require network or filesystem beyond `tmp_path`.
completion_promises.evidence: All 35 tests are pure Pydantic-model construction / method-call / mock assertions. No socket, urllib, requests, httpx, open(), pathlib, or subprocess usage in the file. The autouse _isolate_dotenv fixture in tests/conftest.py already chdirs each test into tmp_path; no test in this file relies on that beyond default isolation. Test suite runs in 0.08s with no external dependencies.
